questions: Q1: Is the 'library' command discoverable from CLI main help?
Q2: Are comment IDs shown in 'comments list' output for use with --reply-to?
Q3: Is the rating alias system intuitive?
Q4: Are error messages helpful when commands fail?
Q5: Is the prior --rating optionality fix for replies still working?

answers.answer: No. The 'library' case exists in src/index.ts switch (lines 94-97) but is NOT listed in the usage() function (lines 37-59). Users running 'hlx' or 'hlx --help' won't discover the library command.
evidence: src/index.ts lines 37-59: usage() does not include any 'hlx library' entry
src/index.ts lines 94-97: 'library' case exists in switch dispatcher

answers.answer: No. The comments-list.ts output format (line 68) shows [rating] author (date): 'content' but omits the comment ID. Users need the comment ID for --reply-to but have no way to discover it from CLI output.
evidence: comments-list.ts line 68: console.log format omits comment.id
comments-post.ts: --reply-to flag expects a comment ID
comments-list.ts type line 6: id: string is in the type but not printed

answers.answer: Yes. RATING_MAP provides 5 aliases: thumbs-up, thumbs-down, love, up, down. The short aliases (up/down) are ergonomic for CLI use. Invalid ratings produce an error listing valid values.
evidence: comments-post.ts lines 5-11: RATING_MAP with all aliases
comments-post.ts lines 47-50: error message lists valid values

answers.answer: Partially. resolve-library-item.ts provides helpful multi-format matching and disambiguation. However, no try-catch blocks around hxFetch calls in comments-list.ts or comments-post.ts — network errors produce raw stack traces.
evidence: resolve-library-item.ts lines 71-76: disambiguation lists matching items
comments-list.ts line 41: hxFetch with no try-catch
comments-post.ts line 62: hxFetch with no try-catch

answers.answer: Yes. comments-post.ts uses conditional logic: getFlag when --reply-to is present, requireFlag when not. Rating is optional for replies, required for top-level posts.
evidence: comments-post.ts lines 29-51: conditional getFlag vs requireFlag based on --reply-to

statement: CLI needs 2 discoverability fixes: (1) Add 'hlx library' commands to main usage() help text in src/index.ts, and (2) Show comment IDs in 'comments list' output so --reply-to is usable. Optional hardening: wrap hxFetch calls in try-catch for user-friendly error messages. Prior --rating optionality fix is working correctly.
